title: You are a Security Analyst. Review the following code changes for OWASP Top 10 and other security concerns. ## TASK Security review of VOICEVOX PNG portrait implementation (6 files). ## KEY AREAS 1. setup-voicevox-portraits.ts: Downloads PNGs via HTTPS from GitHub. Uses https.get with redirect handl...
summary_text: { "score": 2, "vulnerabilities": [ { "severity": "low", "type": "SSRF / untrusted redirect target in downloader", "description": "`scripts/setup-voicevox-portraits.ts` follows HTTP redirects (`res.headers.location`) without validating scheme/host. Although the initial URL is hardcoded, a compromised upstream or malicious redirect could make the script fetch attacker-controlled content from an arbitrary host and store it in `public/characters/*/portrait.png`.", "remediation": "When handling redirects, parse with `new URL(location, currentUrl)`, require `https:`, and allowlist expected hosts (e.g., `raw.githubusercontent.com`). Reject all other redirect targets." }, { "severity": "low", "type": "Input validation / prototype-key lookup risk", "description": "`speakerId` from CLI (`scripts/generate-video.ts`) is propagated to runtime lookups in `voicevox-characters.ts` using a plain object (`CHARACTER_MAP[speakerId]`). Special keys like `__proto__`/`constructor` can hit inherited properties, potentially bypass fallback logic and causing runtime errors (render-time DoS) when code assumes a valid meta object.", "remediation": "Validate `speakerId` against an allowlist (or strict numeric format + known IDs), and gate lookups with `Object.hasOwn(CHARACTER_MAP, speakerId)` or replace `Record` with `Map`." } ], "summary": "No command injection, path traversal, or JSX XSS was found in the reviewed changes. Main concerns are defensive-hardening gaps: unrestricted redirect targets in the PNG downloader and lack of strict `speakerId` validation/own-property checks." }
